goal:
Wave 3 — Three Gaps Assessment. Deliver: L0 assessment Zod schemas (question bank + score request/response/radar); L4 agent-drafted 36-question bank (Likert 1-5 maturity, 3 gaps x 3 sub-dimensions x 4 questions) committed to data/simulators/assessment/questions.json, drafted from usr/07-pre-scaffold-decisions.md structure + knowledge-base Three Gaps content; backend GET /api/assessment/questions + POST /api/assessment/score returning per-gap scores, sub-dimension breakdown, weakest gap, and radar data, with unit tests for the pure scoring function; L8 functional multi-step /assessment wizard UX on apps/web-io (grouped by gap, progress, Likert rows) + results page with a radar chart, weakest-gap callout, per-gap scores, and provisional CTAs to /frameworks/*, /playbook/*, /ask, and the .org reflection site. Anonymous only (no save; save deferred to Wave 4). Keep Lora serif titles + light Geist body + existing site palette, but a purpose-built functional layout (not the editorial article-index chrome; may be wider than the max-w-2xl article column for the radar). See docs/project-progress.md Wave 3 locked decisions.

SHARED CONTRACT (all workers build to this exactly):
- Three gaps (snake_case ids + labels): work_redesign='Work redesign', governance='Governance', value_measurement='Value measurement'.
- Sub-dimensions per gap (4 questions each): work_redesign -> workflow_ownership, metrics, end_to_end_pilots; governance -> autonomy_level, accountability, monitoring; value_measurement -> outcome_hypotheses, multidimensional_roi, board_reporting.
- questions.json shape: { version: string, scale: { min: 1, max: 5, labels: Record<string,string> }, gaps: [{ id, label, description, subDimensions: [{ id, label }] }], questions: [{ id, gap, subDimension, prompt }] } with exactly 36 questions (12 per gap, 4 per sub-dimension). Question ids are stable kebab/snake strings unique across the bank.
- POST /api/assessment/score request: { answers: [{ questionId: string, value: int 1..5 }] }. Must contain an answer for every question id in the bank (all 36); otherwise 400 { ok:false, error }.
- score response (200): { ok:true, overall:number, gaps:[{ id, label, score:number, subDimensions:[{ id, label, score:number }] }], weakestGap:{ id, label, score:number }, radar:[{ axis:string, gap:string, value:number }] }. All scores are averages on the 1-5 scale rounded to 1 decimal. overall = mean of all 36 answers. gap.score = mean of that gap's 12 answers. subDimension.score = mean of its 4 answers. radar value = gap.score (1-5). weakestGap = the gap with the lowest score; tie-break by gap order work_redesign, governance, value_measurement.
summary: Wave 3 Three Gaps assessment: L0 schemas + 36-question bank + scoring API + functional radar wizard on .io
acceptanceCriteria: packages/shared exports Zod schemas + types for the assessment question bank, the score request, and the score response/radar, without removing existing exports
data/simulators/assessment/questions.json holds 36 agent-drafted Likert 1-5 questions (3 gaps x 3 sub-dimensions x 4) and validates against the shared question bank schema

POST /api/assessment/score returns per-gap scores, sub-dimension breakdown, weakest gap, and radar data; invalid/incomplete input returns 400; GET /api/assessment/questions returns the bank; GET /api/health still ok
Unit tests cover the pure scoring function and pass via pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test

/assessment on .io is a functional multi-step wizard (grouped by gap, progress, Likert rows) with a results page showing a radar chart, weakest-gap callout, per-gap scores, and provisional CTAs to /frameworks/*, /playbook/*, /ask, and .org; anonymous only; keeps Lora/Geist + palette
All workspace packages typecheck and apps/web-io builds; the wave ships as a single draft PR to main

tasks.name: backend-foundation
tasks.scopedGoal:
Build the L0 contract, the L4 question bank, and the backend assessment API for Wave 3 of the ai-transformation pnpm+turbo monorepo (apps/backend is Hono; packages/shared is L0 Zod; React 19 / Next 15 frontends live elsewhere and are OUT of scope here). This is the only task touching packages/shared, apps/backend, and data/simulators/assessment. A later frontend task will branch FROM your branch and consume everything you ship, so your exports and JSON shape are a hard contract — follow the SHARED CONTRACT in the plan goal exactly.

1) packages/shared (L0) — edit packages/shared/src/index.ts. KEEP every existing export (healthResponseSchema, contributionSourceSchema, inquiryPayloadSchema, inquiryResponseSchema, createApiClient, SITE_IO, SITE_ORG). ADD, using zod:
- assessmentGapIdSchema = z.enum(['work_redesign','governance','value_measurement']).
- likertValueSchema = z.number().int().min(1).max(5).
- assessmentSubDimensionSchema = z.object({ id: z.string(), label: z.string() }).
- assessmentGapSchema = z.object({ id: assessmentGapIdSchema, label: z.string(), description: z.string(), subDimensions: z.array(assessmentSubDimensionSchema) }).
- assessmentQuestionSchema = z.object({ id: z.string(), gap: assessmentGapIdSchema, subDimension: z.string(), prompt: z.string() }).
- assessmentQuestionBankSchema = z.object({ version: z.string(), scale: z.object({ min: z.literal(1), max: z.literal(5), labels: z.record(z.string(), z.string()) }), gaps: z.array(assessmentGapSchema), questions: z.array(assessmentQuestionSchema) }).
- assessmentAnswerSchema = z.object({ questionId: z.string(), value: likertValueSchema }).
- assessmentScoreRequestSchema = z.object({ answers: z.array(assessmentAnswerSchema).min(1) }).
- assessmentSubScoreSchema = z.object({ id: z.string(), label: z.string(), score: z.number() }).
- assessmentGapScoreSchema = z.object({ id: assessmentGapIdSchema, label: z.string(), score: z.number(), subDimensions: z.array(assessmentSubScoreSchema) }).
- assessmentRadarPointSchema = z.object({ axis: z.string(), gap: assessmentGapIdSchema, value: z.number() }).
- assessmentScoreResponseSchema = z.object({ ok: z.literal(true), overall: z.number(), gaps: z.array(assessmentGapScoreSchema), weakestGap: z.object({ id: assessmentGapIdSchema, label: z.string(), score: z.number() }), radar: z.array(assessmentRadarPointSchema) }).
Export inferred types for each (AssessmentGapId, AssessmentQuestion, AssessmentQuestionBank, AssessmentAnswer, AssessmentScoreRequest, AssessmentScoreResponse, etc.). Extend the object returned by createApiClient(baseUrl) with two methods: getAssessmentQuestions(): Promise<AssessmentQuestionBank> (GET `${base}/api/assessment/questions`, parse with assessmentQuestionBankSchema, throw on non-2xx) and submitAssessmentScore(req: AssessmentScoreRequest): Promise<AssessmentScoreResponse> (POST JSON to `${base}/api/assessment/score`, parse with assessmentScoreResponseSchema, throw on non-2xx). Do NOT import from apps/*. Verify with `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared typecheck`.

2) data/simulators/assessment/questions.json (L4) — author the canonical 36-question bank. READ usr/07-pre-scaffold-decisions.md (the Assessment structure table: gaps, sub-dimensions) and the knowledge-base/ Three Gaps source material (knowledge-base/governance-and-operating-model.md, measuring-ai-value.md, transformation-roadmap.md, what-is-ai-transformation.md, common-pitfalls.md) so the question copy is substantive and grounded, not generic. Write professional Likert maturity statements (respondent rates 1=not in place/ad hoc to 5=systematic/embedded). 12 questions per gap, exactly 4 per sub-dimension, 36 total, matching the SHARED CONTRACT shape and sub-dimension ids. scale.labels should map at least '1','3','5' to short maturity descriptors. The file MUST parse with assessmentQuestionBankSchema. Pretty-print the JSON (2-space indent).

3) apps/backend (L4 scoring) — 
- Create apps/backend/src/lanes/assessment/bank.ts: load + cache the bank from data/simulators/assessment/questions.json using fs, resolving the repo root the same way apps/backend/src/db/index.ts does (path from import.meta.url up to repo root); validate with assessmentQuestionBankSchema and export getQuestionBank(): AssessmentQuestionBank.
- Create apps/backend/src/lanes/assessment/scoring.ts: export a PURE function scoreAssessment(bank: AssessmentQuestionBank, answers: AssessmentAnswer[]): AssessmentScoreResponse implementing the SHARED CONTRACT scoring exactly (per-gap mean of 12, per-sub-dimension mean of 4, overall mean of 36, radar value = gap score, weakestGap = lowest score with tie-break by gap order work_redesign,governance,value_measurement, all scores rounded to 1 decimal). Also export a validation helper that, given the bank + parsed request, returns either { ok:true, answers } or { ok:false, error } — error when any answer references an unknown questionId, when values are out of 1..5 (zod already guards), or when not every bank question id is answered (report e.g. 'All 36 questions must be answered'). Keep scoring pure and import-free of Hono so it is unit-testable.
- Replace apps/backend/src/lanes/assessment/index.ts (currently `export {}`) with a Hono router (default export) exposing GET /questions (returns the bank as JSON 200) and POST /score (parse body with assessmentScoreRequestSchema; on zod failure or the validation helper failure return 400 { ok:false, error }; otherwise return 200 with scoreAssessment(...) result). It will be mounted under /api/assessment, so public paths are GET /api/assessment/questions and POST /api/assessment/score.
- Edit apps/backend/src/index.ts to mount the assessment router: add `import assessmentRouter from './lanes/assessment/index.js';` and `app.route('/api/assessment', assessmentRouter);` WITHOUT removing or altering GET /api/health, the existing harvest mount (app.route('/api', harvestRouter)), or CORS.
- Unit tests: add vitest. Add `vitest` to apps/backend/package.json devDependencies and a `"test": "vitest run"` script. Create apps/backend/vitest.config.ts. Write apps/backend/src/lanes/assessment/scoring.test.ts covering: all-5s answers -> every gap score 5 and overall 5; a mixed case with known hand-computed gap/sub-dimension means and the correct weakestGap; the tie-break ordering when two gaps tie for lowest; and the validation helper rejecting an incomplete answer set and an unknown questionId. Tests must import the real bank via getQuestionBank() (or a small inline fixture that satisfies the schema) and the pure scoreAssessment. Ensure `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend typecheck` still passes (if tsc picks up the test file and complains about vitest globals, either add vitest to the tsconfig types or exclude test files from the backend tsconfig — keep typecheck green) and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` passes.

From repo root ensure `pnpm install` succeeds. DO NOT touch apps/web-io, apps/web-org, or apps/combined. Use crypto.randomUUID only if you need ids (none required here). Verify end to end: build or `tsx`-run the backend with `SQLITE_PATH=/tmp/wave3.db PORT=3001 pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend dev`, then `curl -s localhost:3001/api/assessment/questions` returns the 36-question bank; POST a complete valid answer set to /api/assessment/score and confirm 200 with gaps/weakestGap/radar; POST an incomplete set and confirm 400; confirm GET /api/health still returns ok. Do not open a PR (a later task ships the wave).
acceptance: packages/shared adds the assessment question-bank, score-request, and score-response/radar Zod schemas + inferred types and createApiClient().getAssessmentQuestions()/submitAssessmentScore(), keeping all existing exports; shared typecheck passes
data/simulators/assessment/questions.json contains 36 grounded Likert 1-5 questions (12 per gap, 4 per sub-dimension) that validate against assessmentQuestionBankSchema

GET /api/assessment/questions returns the bank and POST /api/assessment/score returns per-gap + sub-dimension scores, weakest gap, and radar data per the SHARED CONTRACT; incomplete/invalid input returns 400; /api/health and harvest /api/inquiries are untouched
Vitest unit tests for the pure scoreAssessment function (all-5s, a mixed hand-computed case, tie-break, and validation rejection) pass via pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test
pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared typecheck and pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend typecheck both pass; apps/web-io and apps/web-org are untouched
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- From repo root: `pnpm install`.

## Automated
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test`

## Manual
- Start backend: `SQLITE_PATH=/tmp/wave3.db PORT=3001 pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend dev` (wait for listen log).
- `curl -s localhost:3001/api/assessment/questions | head` -> bank with 36 questions.
- POST a complete answer set (one {questionId,value} per bank question, values 1-5) to /api/assessment/score -> 200 with overall, gaps[3] (each with subDimensions[3]), weakestGap, radar[3].
- POST an incomplete set -> HTTP 400 {ok:false,error}.
- `curl -s localhost:3001/api/health` -> {ok:true,...}.

## Gotchas
- The questions.json shape and the shared exports are a hard contract for the downstream frontend task; follow the SHARED CONTRACT in the plan goal exactly. Keep /api/health, CORS, and the harvest mount intact. Keep backend typecheck green even after adding vitest. Load the bank via fs from repo root (mirror db/index.ts), do not hardcode an absolute path.

tasks.name: frontend-assessment
tasks.scopedGoal:
Build the Wave 3 functional assessment UX on apps/web-io (Next 15 App Router, React 19, Tailwind v4). Your clone already contains the backend-foundation work: the assessment Zod schemas/types + createApiClient in packages/shared, the 36-question bank at data/simulators/assessment/questions.json, and the backend GET /api/assessment/questions + POST /api/assessment/score endpoints. This task ONLY edits apps/web-io (and may add components there); do NOT modify packages/shared, apps/backend, apps/web-org, apps/combined, or data/**. Treat the SHARED CONTRACT in the plan goal as the API contract.

Replace the placeholder apps/web-io/app/assessment/page.tsx (keep it a server component exporting metadata { title: 'Assessment' }) so it renders a new client component that drives a multi-step wizard. Build a purpose-built FUNCTIONAL layout — NOT the editorial article chrome. KEEP the site fonts and palette: Lora serif for the main title via the existing `font-serif` class, light Geist body, and the CSS variables in apps/web-io/app/globals.css (var(--background), var(--foreground), var(--accent), var(--muted), var(--card), var(--border)). The assessment container may be wider than the max-w-2xl article column (e.g. max-w-3xl/4xl) so the radar reads well. Study apps/web-io/components/inquiry-form.tsx for the client-component + fetch + accessible-form pattern, apps/web-io/components/site-chrome.tsx and app/globals.css for styling tokens, and apps/web-io/components/theme-toggle.tsx. Assessment is ANONYMOUS in Wave 3 — NO save/login, no localStorage persistence required (save is Wave 4).

Flow (single client state machine: intro -> questions -> results):
1) Intro: short framing of the Three Gaps assessment (36 questions, ~Likert 1-5 maturity, anonymous, ~a few minutes) and a Start button.
2) Questions: fetch the bank from `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL ?? ''}/api/assessment/questions` (use the shared createApiClient or a typed fetch; resolve the base the same way inquiry-form does so prod uses the relative /api proxy and dev can override). Present a STEPPED wizard GROUPED BY GAP (one step/screen per gap, or paged groups) with a visible progress indicator (e.g. step x of 3 and/or % complete). Render each question as an accessible Likert 1-5 row (radio group or segmented control) with clear 1=not in place / 5=systematic anchors from the bank's scale.labels. Use the gap label + description as the step heading. Provide Back/Next navigation; disable Next until the current gap's questions are answered; show inline validation. Track answers in component state keyed by questionId. Handle the questions fetch loading + error states gracefully.
3) On completion, POST { answers } to /api/assessment/score (via shared client submitAssessmentScore or typed fetch). Show a submitting state, and on success transition to the results view with the typed AssessmentScoreResponse held in state. On error show a retry-able message.

Results view:
- A RADAR chart of the three gap scores (1-5). Build a lightweight inline SVG radar/triangle component (one axis per gap) — DO NOT add a new charting dependency; no new runtime packages. Label each axis with the gap label and show its score.
- A weakest-gap callout naming weakestGap.label and its score, with a one-line interpretation.
- Per-gap score cards including the sub-dimension breakdown (subDimensions[].label + score).
- Provisional CTAs (these are placeholders to refine after founder review — keep them clearly grounded but simple). Always offer: Ask a question -> /ask, and reflect with the community -> https://ai-transformation.org. Then map the weakest gap to the most relevant live content: work_redesign -> /frameworks/roadmap and /playbook/patterns; governance -> /frameworks/governance; value_measurement -> /frameworks/measuring-value. Use next/link for internal links. These framework/playbook routes already exist.
- A Restart/Retake option that resets state to the intro.

Accessibility + UX: labelled fieldsets/legends per question group, keyboard-navigable radios, aria-live for step changes/errors, responsive on mobile, works in light and dark mode.

From repo root ensure `pnpm install` succeeds, `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io typecheck` passes, and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build` succeeds. Then run the full-wave integration verification below and OPEN A DRAFT PR to main (base main) titled for Wave 3 summarizing: L0 assessment schemas, the 36-question bank fixture, the scoring API + unit tests, and the functional /assessment wizard + radar results on .io. Mention anonymous-only (save deferred to Wave 4) and that results CTAs are provisional. Do not merge or mark the PR ready. Keep .orchestrate/ out of the PR diff.

Integration verification to run before opening the PR: from repo root `pnpm install`; typecheck shared, backend, and web-io; `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test`; `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build`; start the backend (SQLITE_PATH=/tmp/wave3.db PORT=3001) and run a web-io dev server, open /assessment, complete the wizard, and confirm the POST to /api/assessment/score returns scores that render as a radar + weakest-gap callout + per-gap cards + CTAs.
acceptance: /assessment renders a functional multi-step wizard grouped by gap with a progress indicator and accessible Likert 1-5 rows, keeping Lora title + Geist body + site palette in a purpose-built (non-article) layout
The wizard loads the 36-question bank from GET /api/assessment/questions and POSTs answers to POST /api/assessment/score, handling loading/submitting/error states

The results view shows an inline-SVG radar of the three gap scores, a weakest-gap callout, per-gap score cards with sub-dimension breakdown, and provisional CTAs to /frameworks/*, /playbook/*, /ask, and https://ai-transformation.org, with a retake option; no new runtime dependency is added and assessment stays anonymous (no save)
pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io typecheck and build pass; shared, backend, web-org, combined, and data are untouched

Full-wave verification passes (shared+backend+web-io typecheck, backend test, web-io build, and a live wizard->score->radar smoke against the running backend) and a single draft PR to main summarizing Wave 3 is opened
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- Your branch already contains the backend + shared + data work. `pnpm install` from repo root.

## Automated
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test`
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build`

## Manual
- Start backend: `SQLITE_PATH=/tmp/wave3.db PORT=3001 pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend dev`.
- Start web-io dev (set NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL to the backend origin if not using a proxy) and open /assessment.
- Confirm: intro -> stepped questions grouped by gap with progress -> answering all 36 -> results with an SVG radar, weakest-gap callout, per-gap+sub-dimension cards, and CTAs to /frameworks/*, /playbook/*, /ask, and https://ai-transformation.org. Confirm retake resets. Confirm light/dark both look right.

## Gotchas
- Client component(s) must start with 'use client'; page.tsx stays a server component for metadata. Resolve the API base like inquiry-form. Do NOT add any new runtime dependency (build the radar as inline SVG). Keep the layout functional (not article chrome) but reuse Lora/Geist + palette vars. Keep .orchestrate/ out of the PR.

tasks.name: verify-assessment
tasks.scopedGoal: Independently verify the integrated Wave 3 Three Gaps assessment on the frontend-assessment branch (which contains the full wave: shared schemas, the questions.json fixture, the backend scoring API + tests, and the /assessment UX). Actually run the checks — do not pattern-match. From repo root: `pnpm install`; typecheck shared, backend, and web-io; run `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` and confirm the scoring unit tests pass; `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build`. Confirm data/simulators/assessment/questions.json has exactly 36 questions (12 per gap, 4 per sub-dimension) and validates against the shared assessmentQuestionBankSchema. Start the backend (SQLITE_PATH=/tmp/wave3-verify.db PORT=3001): confirm GET /api/assessment/questions returns the 36-question bank; POST a complete valid answer set to POST /api/assessment/score and confirm 200 with overall, gaps[3] each with subDimensions[3], a weakestGap, and radar[3] consistent with the SHARED CONTRACT scoring (spot-check one gap mean by hand); POST an incomplete set and confirm 400; confirm GET /api/health still returns ok. Inspect apps/web-io/app/assessment to confirm a functional multi-step wizard grouped by gap with progress + Likert rows and a results view rendering an inline-SVG radar, weakest-gap callout, per-gap/sub-dimension cards, and provisional CTAs to /frameworks/*, /playbook/*, /ask, and https://ai-transformation.org, that it keeps Lora/Geist + palette, stays anonymous (no save), and adds no new runtime dependency. Confirm a single draft PR to main exists summarizing Wave 3. Report a Verification verdict with concrete execution evidence (commands run + outcomes) for every frontend-assessment acceptance criterion.
acceptance: Verification section includes concrete execution evidence (commands run + outcomes) for every frontend-assessment acceptance criterion, including backend unit tests passing, web-io build, a live questions->score->radar check, and confirmation the question bank has 36 schema-valid questions
